patterns.what_to_look_for: Upload handler that decides the stored filename/extension/MIME from client-supplied data and writes into a web-served directory, allowing an executable (.php/.jsp/.aspx/.phtml) or double-extension file to be uploaded and then requested back as code.
source_indicators: filename taken from req.file.originalname / part.filename without rename
extension allowlist checked via endsWith() or regex that misses .phtml/.php5/.pht/double-ext
validation on Content-Type header only (file.mimetype) not magic bytes
destination dir inside webroot/static (e.g. public/uploads, multer dest: 'public/')
os.path.join(UPLOAD_DIR, filename) / move_uploaded_file($_FILES['f']['name'])
no execution disable (.htaccess, php_admin_value engine off) on upload dir
blackbox_indicators: upload response returns a fetchable URL under same app origin/webroot
shell.php.jpg, shell.phtml, shell%00.jpg, or trailing-dot/space filenames accepted
Content-Type: image/jpeg spoof accepted for a non-image body
GET of uploaded path returns it executed (X-Powered-By/interpreter behavior) not as text/plain
evidence_required: request showing the malicious filename/Content-Type accepted (2xx + stored URL)
GET of stored file proving server-side execution of a benign unique marker (e.g. echo of a nonce)
stored path is within an interpreter-enabled, web-served directory
false_positive_checks: file is re-named to a random UUID and extension is forced server-side
stored outside webroot or on object storage with no interpreter
served only with Content-Disposition: attachment + X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
magic-byte/content sniffing validation rejects the spoofed payload
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Upload a benign file named probe.<nonce>.phtml (or double-ext like .php.jpg) whose body echoes a unique marker, then GET the returned URL and check whether the marker is interpreted vs served verbatim.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as RCE/webshell (Critical) only if execution of the benign marker is proven; if accepted but served inert, report as unrestricted-upload misconfiguration (Medium).

patterns.what_to_look_for: User-uploaded active-content files (SVG, HTML, XML, MHTML) served inline from the application's own origin with a renderable Content-Type and no CSP/sandbox, yielding stored XSS in the authenticated origin.
source_indicators: image upload allowlist includes svg/svg+xml or html/xhtml
files served with res.type from stored extension and disposition 'inline'
no Content-Security-Policy header on the file-serving route
avatar/attachment served from main app domain not a sandboxed/cookieless host
no SVG sanitization (DOMPurify/svg-sanitizer) before store
blackbox_indicators: uploaded .svg returned with Content-Type: image/svg+xml and Content-Disposition: inline
SVG containing <script>/onload executes when the file URL is opened directly
files served from app origin sharing session cookies, no sandbox/CSP
HTML upload rendered as text/html rather than downloaded
evidence_required: uploaded SVG/HTML with a benign DOM marker (e.g. sets document.title to a nonce)
headless/browser load of the file URL showing the script ran in the app's origin
response headers proving inline rendering on a cookie-bearing origin
false_positive_checks: served from a separate sandbox domain (usercontent.*) with no app cookies
Content-Disposition: attachment forces download instead of render
CSP script-src 'none'/'self' nonce blocks inline/embedded script
SVG/HTML sanitized or rasterized to PNG on upload
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Upload an SVG/HTML containing a benign script that writes a unique nonce into the DOM (no exfil), open its served URL in a headless browser, and confirm the nonce executed under the app origin.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as stored XSS scoped to the serving origin; severity rises if that origin holds the session/auth cookies (account takeover reachable).

patterns.what_to_look_for: Server-side archive extraction (zip/tar/jar/gzip) that writes entries using the archive-supplied entry name without canonicalizing/confining the output path, enabling Zip-Slip path traversal (or zip-bomb resource exhaustion).
source_indicators: ZipInputStream/zipfile.extractall/tarfile.extractall(path) over whole archive
new File(destDir, entry.getName()) / os.path.join(dest, member.name) without normalize+startsWith check
entry name not validated for ../ or absolute/drive-letter prefixes
no per-entry size/total-size/entry-count limit before extraction
symlink entries in tar followed during extraction
blackbox_indicators: feature ingests user archives (import, theme/plugin upload, bulk import, restore)
archive with an entry named ../../<file> is accepted without error
extraction time/size grows disproportionately for a tiny crafted archive (bomb)
evidence_required: crafted archive with a single benign entry named ../<nonce>.txt
read-back showing the marker file landed outside the intended extraction dir
source path showing entry name joined to dest without confinement
false_positive_checks: extractor normalizes path and rejects entries escaping the target root
uses safe API (e.g. Commons-Compress with canonical check, Python 3.12 tarfile filter='data')
entry count/size caps enforced before write
extraction sandboxed to a temp dir later validated, never the live tree
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Upload an archive containing one benign entry named ../zipslip-<nonce>.txt and a normal file, then check (via a known read path or error/log) whether the marker escaped the extraction directory.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as arbitrary file write / path traversal (High-Critical) when escape is confirmed; note overwrite-of-critical-file or webshell-drop chaining potential.

patterns.what_to_look_for: Download/export/preview endpoint that builds a filesystem path or storage key from a user-controlled parameter (file=, path=, name=, id=, key=) and reads it back without canonicalization or tenant scoping, giving path traversal or cross-tenant file read.
source_indicators: res.sendFile(req.query.file) / open(BASE + request.args['name']) / readFile(path.join(dir, userInput))
decodeURIComponent applied then path joined, no realpath+startsWith confinement
storage key built from raw param (s3.getObject({Key: userKey})) with no owner check
sequential/numeric document id used to fetch file with no authorization filter
blackbox_indicators: download?file=../../../../etc/passwd or url-encoded %2e%2e%2f returns file contents
absolute path or UNC/drive-letter accepted (file=/etc/hostname, file=C:\)
incrementing/guessing a document id returns another account's file
nested/double encoding (..%252f) bypasses a naive filter
evidence_required: request with traversal/alternate id and the returned out-of-scope file body
for IDOR: two accounts proving account B reads account A's file
source path showing user input reaching the read sink uncanonicalized/unscoped
false_positive_checks: path canonicalized (realpath) and asserted to stay under the allowed root
param is an opaque indirect id mapped server-side, not a path
ownership/tenant predicate applied to the file lookup
access via signed, scoped, expiring URLs only
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Request the download endpoint with ../ and url-encoded traversal toward a known benign in-app file (e.g. package.json or /etc/hostname), and separately fetch a neighboring document id as a second account, observing whether out-of-scope content returns.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as path traversal / arbitrary file read or BOLA on stored files; rate by sensitivity of reachable content (secrets, other tenants' documents).

patterns.what_to_look_for: Server-side parser invoked on uploaded files (XML/Office OOXML/SVG/SVGZ/PDF/image via ImageMagick/ffmpeg) with insecure defaults, enabling XXE (file read/SSRF/OOB) or library command-injection during parse.
source_indicators: DocumentBuilderFactory/SAXParser/XMLReader without disabling DOCTYPE/external entities
lxml etree with resolve_entities=True / no_network not set; Python xml without defusedxml
ImageMagick `convert`/`mogrify` or ffmpeg shelled out on raw upload (ImageTragick, MSL/HTTPS coders)
office/spreadsheet (POI, openpyxl, docx) parsing untrusted docs with entity expansion on
EXIF/metadata extraction via exiftool/exec on filename
blackbox_indicators: DOCX/XLSX/SVG/XML upload with an external entity triggers an OOB DNS/HTTP callback to a collaborator host
parsed-document response or error reflects local file content (/etc/passwd) via XXE
delay/SSRF when entity points at an internal URL or cloud metadata IP
billion-laughs style entity expansion spikes CPU/memory on parse
evidence_required: uploaded document carrying an external entity pointing to a controlled OAST/collaborator host
out-of-band interaction (DNS/HTTP) logged, or local-file content reflected in output
source confirming the parser runs with DTD/external entities or unsafe coder enabled
false_positive_checks: parser hardened: FEATURE_SECURE_PROCESSING / disallow-doctype-decl / external entities disabled, or defusedvxml
ImageMagick policy.xml disables vulnerable coders / network
parsing sandboxed (no egress, no fs) and resource-capped
no OOB callback and no file reflection on a hardened build
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Upload an XLSX/SVG/XML containing an external entity referencing a unique controlled OAST hostname and watch the collaborator for an OOB DNS/HTTP hit (no local-file exfil needed to confirm).
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report XXE as file-read/SSRF/OOB (High-Critical by reachable internal surface); report parser command-injection as RCE; cite the specific parser/library and unsafe setting.

patterns.what_to_look_for: Stored uploads (avatars, invoices, ID docs, exports) served from a predictable/sequential/enumerable path with no per-request authorization check, so any user or anonymous client can read or overwrite other users' files.
source_indicators: static-file middleware mounted directly on the upload dir (app.use('/uploads', static(dir)))
stored filename = original name, sequential counter, or md5(email) — predictable
serving route lacks the auth/ownership middleware applied elsewhere
writes use a fixed/predictable destination key allowing overwrite of an existing object
no signed-URL / expiry on object-storage access
blackbox_indicators: /uploads/<n>.pdf or /files/<incrementing-id> returns another user's document
files reachable unauthenticated (no cookie) on direct URL
low-entropy/guessable names; switching ?user= or sequential ids leaks content
re-upload with a known key replaces/overwrites an existing stored file
evidence_required: as a low-priv/anonymous client, request another account's predictable file URL and receive its content
demonstration of enumeration (sequential ids) or overwrite of a known key
source/headers showing the serving route has no ownership check
false_positive_checks: filenames are high-entropy random UUIDs and URLs are signed + short-lived
serving route enforces an ownership/tenant predicate before streaming
writes use unique non-colliding keys (no overwrite)
files require an authenticated, scoped session that 403s cross-account
patterns.suggested_validation_task: As an anonymous or second low-priv account, request a predictable/neighboring stored-file URL (sequential id or guessable name) and confirm whether content for an account you don't own is returned.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as broken access control on stored files (BOLA / unauth file disclosure), or file-overwrite when a predictable key is writable; severity by data sensitivity.
